checklist.description: IncidentRecord type includes fields for: what happened, root cause, fix applied, and watch-for guidance
checklist.name: Episodic entry format

checklist.description: classifyIncident or guide covers at least 5 of: broken artifacts, build failures, workarounds, misleading errors, API drift, config root causes, user frustration, invariant violations
checklist.name: Incident trigger categories

checklist.description: Guide states that invariant violations and manual artifact repairs must be documented before continuing with the rest of the task, not deferred to session end
checklist.name: Immediate capture rule

checklist.description: shouldRecordIncident or guide mentions that spending more than ~2 minutes investigating is a signal that an incident is worth recording
checklist.name: Investigation time heuristic

checklist.description: Guide distinguishes at least 4 entry types (procedural, semantic, episodic, reflection) with clear when-to-use guidance
checklist.name: Entry type differentiation

checklist.description: Metadata or guide specifies that every record must include branch:<branch> and at least one scope:<...> tag
checklist.name: Branch and scope tags

checklist.description: IncidentRecord includes refs field with 1-5 references to files, tools, services, or endpoints where the incident occurred
checklist.name: Refs metadata

checklist.description: Guide states that episodic entries are generally unsigned, while identity/soul entries should always be signed
checklist.name: Signing guidance

checklist.description: incident-relations.ts supports at least 3 of: caused_by, supports, contradicts, references as relation types
checklist.name: Relation types

checklist.description: Relations have explicit direction (source entry → target entry) rather than being bidirectional
checklist.name: Directed relations

checklist.description: Metadata includes operator (human user) and tool (AI coding environment) fields
checklist.name: Operator and tool fields

checklist.description: Guide or code defaults to semantic when entry type is uncertain
checklist.name: Default entry type

context: Tests whether the agent correctly implements episodic entry creation patterns: incident categories that trigger recording, immediate-capture rule, structured episodic format, entry type selection, metadata conventions, tagging, refs, content signing guidance, and relation types for linking incidents.
